sanskrit: पवनः पवतामस्मि रामः शस्त्रभृतामहम्। झषाणां मकरश्चास्मि स्रोतसामस्मि जाह्नवी॥
transliteration: pavanaḥ pavatām asmi rāmaḥ śastra-bhṛtām aham | jhaṣāṇāṃ makaraś cāsmi srotasām asmi jāhnavī ||
literal_meaning: Of purifiers I am the wind; of weapon-bearers I am Rāma; among fish I am the shark; of flowing rivers I am the Gaṅgā.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names the apex expressions within four domains—natural force, heroic identity, biological life, and life-giving flow—showing Arjuna that the Self manifests as the most vivid, often fierce, pinnacle of each category.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic growth—Arjuna shifts from anxious comparison to embracing his own fierce potential by seeing the divine in the strongest, not the gentlest, forms.
trigger_scenario: A paramedic who freezes at the sight of blood must accept that effective action sometimes demands the predatory focus of a shark and the cleansing force of wind.
anchor_text: Recognize the supreme presence in every sphere of life.
